name: Server Logs
description: Read and scan server logs for errors, warnings, or unusual patterns. Admin/owner only.
systemPromptAddition:
## Server logs

You can inspect the OpenEnsemble server logs to help the admin diagnose issues. Use `read_logs` to fetch recent entries (optionally filtered by file, level, or keyword) and `scan_for_concerns` to get a high-signal summary of errors and repeated warnings.

When the admin asks vague questions like 'is everything OK?', 'anything concerning in the logs?', or 'why did X fail?', default to `scan_for_concerns` first — it returns a digest instead of raw lines. Report findings plainly: counts of each issue class, the first/last time each occurred, and a sentence of impact. Don't dump raw JSON unless asked.

Logs never contain message bodies or prompts — only metadata. If the admin needs deeper evidence, point them at the specific file/timestamp rather than trying to reconstruct content.

function.name: read_logs
function.description: Read recent entries from the server log. Returns newest-last. Admin/owner only. Use this when the user asks to 'see the logs', 'show recent errors', or wants to inspect a specific time window or keyword.

file.description: Which log file to read. 'app' is everything; 'error' is errors-only (faster to scan).

level.description: Filter by level. Omit for all.

since.description: ISO timestamp or epoch-ms; only return entries at or after this time.

function.name: scan_for_concerns
function.description: Scan recent log entries and return a digest of issues worth attention: errors, repeated warnings, auth failures, provider/tool failures. Groups by tag + message so the user sees counts and timespans rather than raw lines. Use this for open-ended questions like 'anything wrong?' or 'what's been failing lately?'.

since.description: ISO timestamp or epoch-ms. Default: last 24h.

maxGroups.description: Max distinct issue groups to return (default 15).
